outline.title: Equal Justice Under Law — But Is It Equal in Practice?
outline.summary: Introduction to the central research question regarding legal representation and sentencing.

outline.title: A Comprehensive, Publicly Verifiable Dataset
outline.summary: Overview of the USSC FY 2024 dataset and key variables used.

outline.title: Legal Representation Sits at the Crossroads of Race and Sentencing
outline.summary: Visual explanation of the mediation pathway model.
outline.image_plan: /home/ubuntu/legal_literacy_justice_project/outputs/whitepaper/fig5_conceptual_framework.png - Image 10 is a direct match for the 'Conceptual Framework Diagram' mentioned in the slide content.

outline.title: Hispanic Defendants Are Sentenced to Prison at a Rate 29% Higher Than White Defendants
outline.summary: Data visualization showing racial disparities in prison sentence rates.
outline.image_plan: /home/ubuntu/legal_literacy_justice_project/outputs/whitepaper/fig1_prison_rate_by_race.png - Image 9 is 'Figure 1 - Prison Sentence Rate by Race/Ethnicity bar chart' as referenced in the slide content.

outline.title: Defendants Without Private Attorneys Serve an Average of 35 More Months in Prison
outline.summary: Data visualization showing sentence length disparities by attorney type.
outline.image_plan: /home/ubuntu/legal_literacy_justice_project/outputs/models/roc_curve_prison.png - Image 2 shows average sentence length by attorney type, which directly supports the slide's content about attorney type predicting sentence length.

outline.title: Three Distinct Models Tested for Both Accuracy and Fairness
outline.summary: Explanation of the three modeling approaches used (Logistic, Multilevel, Mediation).

outline.title: All Three Models Achieve Strong Predictive Accuracy
outline.summary: Comparison of model accuracy and AUC-ROC scores.
outline.image_plan: /home/ubuntu/legal_literacy_justice_project/outputs/fairness/fairness_metrics_comparison.png - Image 3 is an ROC curve, which represents model performance and accuracy, aligning with the slide's content.

outline.title: The Multilevel Model Is the Only Model to Pass the Standard Disparate Impact Test
outline.summary: Fairness metrics comparison showing the superiority of the Multilevel Model.
outline.image_plan: /home/ubuntu/legal_literacy_justice_project/outputs/fairness/summary_comparison_slide.png - Image 7 directly compares fairness metrics for Logistic Regression vs. Multilevel Model, which is the core of this slide.

outline.title: Accounting for Geographic Context Reduces Racial Bias by Over 37%
outline.summary: Explanation of why the Multilevel Model is fairer (zip code justice).

outline.title: The Data Reveals a System Where Access to Counsel Shapes the Scales of Justice
outline.summary: Key discussion points on representation, bias, and model fairness.

outline.title: Data-Driven Evidence Supports Three Actionable Policy Reforms
outline.summary: Three concrete policy recommendations based on findings.

outline.title: This Study Is a Foundation, Not a Final Word
outline.summary: Discussion of study limitations and future research directions.

outline.title: The Scales of Justice Can Be Recalibrated
outline.summary: Concluding thoughts on the role of data science in justice reform.
